content:
# Nitrate

**Summary**: A nitrogen compound that can contaminate groundwater from agricultural or industrial sources.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] investigated nitrate concentrations and their isotopic signatures in the Wasia aquifer.

- [[2020-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2020]] monitored nitrate as a primary degradation product of ammonia.

## Scientific Context

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] Used as an indicator of agricultural runoff and waste contamination.

- [[2020-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2020]] monitored nitrate as a primary degradation product of ammonia.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019]] Ion chromatography and isotopic analysis.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Nitrate levels were found to be high, with 100% of samples exceeding drinking limits for several parameters related to its presence. ([[2019-ahmed-2|Ahmed, 2019, p. 41]])


- Nitrate concentrations reached 73.3 mg/L at the end of a 3-hour run. ([[2020-ghazwani|Ghazwani, 2020, p. 65]])
